methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Atomic Industries is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Aaron Slodov and Lou Young Jr., having raised $25M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of nine private and one public robotics firm, including Series B peers 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and Fairmat ($350M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $25M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yields $50M. Peer-median analysis across the six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The peer-median approach reflects substantial dispersion in the robotics sector, driven largely by heterogeneity in technical capability, market traction, and funding trajectory.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $42M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $562M. The low bound anchors to stage-based progression; the high bound reflects peer multiples at the upper quartile. Material uncertainties include the degree to which Atomic's technical differentiation aligns with the highest-valued comparables versus mid-market peers, and the timing and magnitude of revenue inflection relative to Series B fundraising. The wide peer range indicates sector-specific valuation volatility at this stage.
